stories.title: Garden of the Gods Family Water Feature Coming Summer 2026
stories.angle: The City of Colorado Springs has announced a new water attraction at Garden of the Gods Park — a lazy river-style water feature offering families a unique way to experience the park's iconic red rock formations. Construction begins spring 2026 with a summer 2026 opening target. The announcement comes as COS parks officials respond to rising summer temperatures and demand for family-friendly outdoor options.

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
COS families: Garden of the Gods is getting a water feature this summer. A lazy river-style attraction through the red rock formations. Construction starts this spring. More details at coloradosprings.gov/parks.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Plan ahead: Garden of the Gods' new water attraction is scheduled to open before summer 2026. A new way to cool off while exploring Colorado Springs' most iconic park with your family.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
Something exciting is coming to one of Colorado Springs' most beloved parks this summer — and families are going to love it.

The City of Colorado Springs has announced a new water feature at Garden of the Gods Park. The attraction is described as a lazy river-style experience that will wind through the park's iconic red rock formations, giving visitors a unique, cooling way to enjoy the geology that makes Garden of the Gods so special. It's designed with family-friendly features, designated entry points, and scenic floating areas.

Construction is scheduled to begin this spring, with the goal of opening before summer 2026.

If you've been to Garden of the Gods with your family, you know the summer heat can be intense. This addition is a direct response to rising temperatures and the demand for outdoor options that keep families comfortable while still connecting them to COS's natural landscape.

We'll share more details as they're announced — including pricing and access info — but for now, it's worth adding to the summer bucket list. Visit coloradosprings.gov/parks for updates.

What other improvements would you love to see at COS parks?
facebook.image_caption: Garden of the Gods is getting a water feature this summer. A lazy river-style attraction through the red rock formations — a new way to experience Colorado Springs' most iconic park with your family. Construction starts this spring.

stories.title: CPSC Recall — Vevor Baby Swings — Suffocation Hazard
stories.angle: Sanven Technology has recalled approximately 1,020 Vevor Baby Swings due to a suffocation hazard. The swings were marketed for infant sleep but have an incline angle greater than 10 degrees, violating the mandatory standard for infant sleep products and the ban on inclined sleepers under the Safe Sleep for Babies Act. Sold Jan-Aug 2025 on Amazon and Vevor.com for $65-$80. Models: BB501K, BB702A, BB005K. Remedy: full refund. Call 855-599-6320.

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
CPSC recall: Vevor Baby Swings (models BB501K, BB702A, BB005K). Inclined sleeper violation — suffocation risk. Sold on Amazon + vevor.com Jan–Aug 2025, $65–$80. Stop using now. Full refund: 855-599-6320.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
If you bought a Vevor baby swing on Amazon or Vevor.com between January and August 2025, please check this now.

Sanven Technology has recalled approximately 1,020 Vevor Baby Swings due to a suffocation hazard. The swings were marketed and sold for infant sleep — but they have an incline angle greater than 10 degrees, which violates the Safe Sleep for Babies Act and the mandatory standard for infant sleep products. Inclined surfaces can cause an infant's head to tip forward, blocking the airway.

Here's what to check: the affected models are BB501K (white/gray with white base), BB702A (white/gray with gray base), and BB005K (black/gray with black base). The swings sold for $65 to $80.

If you have one of these: stop using it immediately and take it away from your baby. Contact Sanven Technology for a full refund. They can be reached at 855-599-6320 from 9 AM to 5 PM PT, Monday through Friday.

The Safe Sleep for Babies Act bans inclined sleepers because of the real risk they pose to infants. This recall is exactly why that law exists.

Have you seen this recall shared in your parent groups? Pass this along to anyone with an infant who might have this swing.
facebook.image_caption: CPSC recall: Vevor Baby Swings (models BB501K, BB702A, BB005K). Inclined sleeper violation — suffocation risk. Sold on Amazon and Vevor.com from January to August 2025 for $65-$80. Stop using now and call 855-599-6320 for a full refund.

article.headline: Vevor Baby Swing Recall: Check These Model Numbers Now

stories.title: CPSC Recall — Zippee Silicone Activity Toys — Choking Hazard
stories.angle: Mobi Games has recalled approximately 117,500 Zippee silicone activity toys due to a choking hazard. The silicone cylinder toys have spherical ends that can reach the back of a child's throat. One incident: a child placed the toy in their mouth and choked. Sold Nov 2019-Jan 2024 on Amazon, PlayMobi, and specialty toy stores for about $20. Remedy: full refund from Mobi Games.

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Check your toy box: CPSC recall on Zippee silicone activity toys (teal cylinder, colorful strings). Choking hazard — 117,500 units. Sold on Amazon + toy stores, 2019–2024, ~$20. Full refund from Mobi Games.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
This one might be sitting in your toy box right now — and you might not even know it's been recalled.

Mobi Games has recalled approximately 117,500 Zippee silicone activity toys due to a choking hazard. The Zippee is a teal flexible silicone cylinder with holes where six colorful silicone strings can be pulled. Five of those six strings have a spherical ball end. The problem: those spherical ends can reach the back of a child's throat, creating a serious choking hazard. There has been one reported incident of a child placing the toy in their mouth and choking.

The toys were sold from November 2019 through January 2024 — which means they've been in homes for years. Families may have moved the toy from one child to a younger sibling without realizing a recall had been issued.

Here's how to identify the toy: look for date codes in MMDDYY format ranging from 030620 to 110823 stamped inside the silicone cylinder body. If you have one, stop letting your children use it immediately and contact Mobi Games for a full refund. You can find recall information at cpsc.gov or at playmobi.com/pages/product-recall.

The Zippee was available on Amazon, PlayMobi's website, and in specialty toy stores for around $20. If you still have yours, the refund is worth the 10-minute process.

Does this ring a bell? If you have one, share this post so other families can check too.
facebook.image_caption: CPSC recall: Zippee silicone activity toys — 117,500 units. Choking hazard from spherical ends. Sold on Amazon and toy stores 2019-2024 for about $20. Full refund from Mobi Games. Check your toy box.

stories.title: COS April Family Events — Sensory Inclusion Night + Spring Programs
stories.angle: Two upcoming COS family events not covered in yesterday's run: (1) Sensory Inclusion Night at Widefield Parks & Recreation on April 16, 5:00-6:30 PM — designed for families of kids with sensory needs; (2) Family Painting P-Art-Y at Widefield on April 22, 6:30-8:30 PM — Underwater Coral Watercolor. Also: Sertich Ice Center has opened spring Learn to Skate registration for all ages and skill levels.

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
COS families: Sensory Inclusion Night at Widefield Parks & Rec this Thursday (April 16, 5–6:30 PM). Also: spring Learn to Skate registration is now open at Sertich Ice Center.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
A few COS family events coming up that are worth putting on your calendar this week.

First, one that not enough families know about: Sensory Inclusion Night at Widefield Parks & Recreation on Thursday, April 16 from 5:00 to 6:30 PM. If you have a child who benefits from sensory-aware environments, this event is designed for your family. These kinds of dedicated inclusive spaces are worth supporting — and attending.

The week after, on April 22, Widefield Parks & Recreation is hosting a Family Painting P-Art-Y: Underwater Coral Watercolor from 6:30 to 8:30 PM. A fun option for families who want a creative evening activity together.

And for families thinking about year-round activities: Sertich Ice Center has opened spring registration for its Learn to Skate program, which offers classes for all ages and skill levels. It's a great way to try something new while the weather outside can still be unpredictable.

For more event details and registration links, visit coloradosprings.gov/recreation and parksandrec.wsd3.org.

Which of these is your family most excited about?
facebook.image_caption: This Thursday: Sensory Inclusion Night at Widefield Parks & Rec (April 16, 5-6:30 PM). Plus: Family Painting P-Art-Y on April 22 and spring skating registration now open at Sertich Ice Center. Colorado Springs spring events are filling up.

stories.title: D20 Spring 2026: School Choice Open, Spring Calendar Reminders
stories.angle: Academy District 20 (D20) families have several spring action items: School Choice applications for non-resident families for the 2026-27 school year are currently open (families without a current D20 enrollment can apply for remaining seats). D20 has maintained Accredited with Distinction status for 17 consecutive years. Spring semester winding down — families should check district calendar at asd20.org.

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
D20 families and those considering Academy District 20: School Choice applications for 2026-27 are open now for non-resident families. 17 years of Accredited with Distinction. Apply at asd20.org.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
A reminder for Colorado Springs families considering Academy District 20 for the 2026-27 school year: School Choice applications are open now.

D20's School Choice program allows families who don't currently have a child enrolled in Academy District 20 to apply for available seats at D20 schools. If you've been curious about D20's programs, this is the window. Visit asd20.org for the application and to see which schools have available seats.

For families already in D20, spring is a good time to review your school calendar and make sure you're connected to your school's communication channels. The 2025-26 year is winding down, and the transition to a new school year — or a new school — takes more planning than most of us expect.

A quick note on D20's track record: the district has been Accredited with Distinction by the State of Colorado for 17 consecutive years. It serves over 26,000 students at 40 schools across Colorado Springs. That's a strong foundation for families considering a school change for fall.

Questions about D20 enrollment? Drop them in the comments or visit asd20.org directly.
facebook.image_caption: D20 School Choice for 2026-27 is open now to non-resident families. Academy District 20 has been Accredited with Distinction for 17 consecutive years. Visit asd20.org to apply or learn more.
